topic: Why Gyms Want You to Never Show Up

packaging.title: Why Gyms Want You to Never Show Up
altTitles: The Real Reason Gyms Are Cheap
How Gyms Survive on Empty Treadmills
packaging.description: An explanation of the gym membership business model, why low monthly fees depend on members not attending, and how overselling capacity keeps fitness chains profitable.

thumbnail.visualIdea: a gym membership card overlaid on a nearly empty treadmill row

packaging.hookPromise: Your $10 gym is praying you don't come — because it sold 6,500 memberships for a building that only fits 300 people at once.
openLoops: How can a gym charge so little and stay open?
What's the magic number of members who actually show up?
Why do gyms make canceling so hard?
packaging.payoff: Major chains sign up roughly 6,500 members per location that physically holds about 300, betting on a no-show rate above 80% — the entire pricing model collapses if even half of members actually used what they paid for.
packaging.whyItWorks: Nearly everyone has an unused gym membership and feels personally implicated in the reveal.
